html:
Daily Economic Briefing

Monday, 13 July 2026

MOVE 66.79 ElevatedSOFR 3.58% StableNFCI −0.515 LooseT-bills 11:30 ET
📊 Liquidity & Stress Metrics — The "Gnosis" Pulse
MetricValueMarket Context
MOVE Index66.79Latest available close (10 Jul). Treasury volatility is below last week's peak but remains elevated: term-premium and inflation surprises can still move ES through yields.
SOFR Rate3.58%Latest published New York Fed reference rate (8 Jul). Secured overnight funding remains orderly; no clear plumbing stress signal.
NFCI−0.515Latest Chicago Fed reading (week ending 3 Jul). Negative NFCI means broad financial conditions remain looser than their historical average.
Gnosis Interpretation
The liquidity backdrop is still supportive, but it is not a low-volatility backdrop. Calm SOFR and loose NFCI favor risk appetite; MOVE says to respect yield sensitivity. Today's bill supply and fiscal statement are secondary catalysts before Tuesday's CPI and Fed Chair testimony reset the macro tape.
📅 Economic Calendar: 2026-07-13
Investing.com's U.S. two-/three-star calendar was used for discovery and cross-checking. Treasury auction dates were verified against Treasury's tentative schedule. No BLS, BEA, Census, EIA weekly inventory, scheduled Fed speaker, or FOMC event is due today.
EventETGMT+8Notes
13- & 26-Week Treasury Bill Auctions11:3023:30Treasury's published schedule confirms both auctions today, settling 16 Jul. Watch bid-to-cover and dealer take-up as a clean read on front-end demand.
Treasury Large Position Report Deadline12:0000:00 (+1)Reporting deadline for a called Treasury FRN position. Operational rather than a routine macro print, but a useful Treasury-market structure flag.
Monthly Treasury Statement, June14:0002:00 (+1)Monthly federal budget data. Focus on receipts, outlays, and the deficit narrative; usually lower immediate ES impact than the coming CPI release.
🏛️ Geopolitical & Fed Watch
Tuesday Risk Transfer
June CPI is scheduled for 08:30 ET tomorrow, followed by Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's House testimony at 10:00 ET. Light Monday liquidity can exaggerate positioning ahead of that inflation-and-policy sequence.
Energy & Market Structure
No scheduled EIA inventory report today; the next regular petroleum-status release is Wednesday. Monitor crude and long-end yields together: falling oil with stable yields supports risk; oil and yields rising together keeps the MOVE warning active.
📈 Trading Strategy Note (ES Futures)
Base case: Treat Monday as a positioning session, not a clean macro-breakout day. Supportive funding conditions favor buying accepted pullbacks, but avoid chasing thin-liquidity impulses.
Key window: Reassess after 11:30 ET bill results and 14:00 ET fiscal data. The larger decision is whether ES holds value into Tuesday's CPI rather than the statement itself.
Invalidation: If crude, long yields, and MOVE rise together, reduce size and favor responsive mean reversion. Do not let loose NFCI override an active rates-volatility signal.
